canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, visual storytelling is consistently defined as the task of generating a coherent narrative or story based on a sequence of images. This behavior is operationalized and measured using the VIST benchmark, as one paper explicitly references "visual storytelling from VIST". The prevailing usage positions it as a task where a model receives a sequence of images as input and produces a textual story as output. A less common framing, found in one paper, describes it as an application that requires the "simultaneous generation of both images and text" in an interleaved format. It is presented as a capability on which models are evaluated for performance and is studied alongside other multi-image tasks such as "spotting the difference".
papers: Interleaved Scene Graphs for Interleaved Text-and-Image Generation Assessment
LLaVA-Interleave: Tackling Multi-image, Video, and 3D in Large Multimodal Models
MMIE: Massive Multimodal Interleaved Comprehension Benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models
Modality-Specialized Synergizers for Interleaved Vision-Language Generalists
MaVEn: An Effective Multi-granularity Hybrid Visual Encoding Framework for Multimodal Large Language Model
Voila-A: Aligning Vision-Language Models with User's Gaze Attention
